requiredExam.label: Inspect feet for ulcers

source.source_section: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate diabetic neuropathy, foot ulcer risk, infection, or peripheral arterial disease. An ulcer or wound changes infection workup, offloading, antibiotic planning, and disposition.
requiredExam.rationale: Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect both feet including interdigital spaces and pressure points for ulcer, callus, erythema, drainage, deformity, or infection.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When established diabetes, neuropathy, infection, vascular disease, or foot symptoms are relevant
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: diabetic neuropathy, foot ulcer risk, infection, or peripheral arterial disease
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: An ulcer or wound changes infection workup, offloading, antibiotic planning, and disposition.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect feet for ulcers is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Palpate pedal pulses

requiredExam.technique: Palpate bilateral pulses at the named sites, comparing amplitude, symmetry, rhythm, and perfusion.

requiredExam.diagnostic_target: diabetic foot perfusion, peripheral arterial disease, or vascular risk

requiredExam.management_change: Diminished pulses change vascular risk assessment and foot-wound management.
requiredExam.limitations: Palpate pedal pulses is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Test monofilament sensation

source.source_section: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate diabetic neuropathy, foot ulcer risk, infection, or peripheral arterial disease. Loss of protective sensation changes foot-care counseling, footwear planning, and ulcer prevention.
requiredExam.rationale: Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.
requiredExam.technique: Apply a 10-g monofilament perpendicular to standard plantar sites until it bends, with eyes closed, documenting sites felt or missed.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When neuropathy risk, established diabetes duration, foot symptoms, or fall risk is relevant
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: diabetic neuropathy, foot ulcer risk, infection, or peripheral arterial disease
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Loss of protective sensation changes foot-care counseling, footwear planning, and ulcer prevention.
requiredExam.limitations: Test monofilament sensation is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.

requiredExam.label: Measure blood pressure

source.source_section: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus curated endocrine workup: basic bedside data and safety checks
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context
requiredExam.management_change: Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
requiredExam.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.technique: Measure blood pressure using an appropriately sized cuff after a brief rest period, seated or supine as clinically appropriate.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.

conditionalExam.management_change: A wound changes infection workup, antibiotics, and disposition.
conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect foot wound is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration

source.source_section: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus curated endocrine workup: conditional bedside maneuver add-ons
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.action: Add when vomiting, dehydration, poor intake, polyuria, hyperglycemia is present; this evaluates volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion. Dehydration changes fluid strategy, lab urgency, and monitoring needs.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When vomiting, poor intake, polyuria, hyperglycemia, hypercalcemia, or hypernatremia is present
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Dehydration changes fluid strategy, lab urgency, and monitoring needs.
conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

reference_values: Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.
Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis.
UACR normal <30 mg/g; moderately increased 30-300; severely increased >300.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: Diagnose with A1c >=6.5%, fasting plasma glucose >=126 mg/dL, 2-hour OGTT >=200 mg/dL, or random glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms; repeat confirmatory testing if asymptomatic.
At diagnosis, classify ASCVD, heart failure, CKD, obesity, hypoglycemia risk, medication access, and pregnancy possibility before selecting therapy.
Screen complications with eGFR, urine albumin-creatinine ratio, lipids, BP, foot neuropathy/perfusion exam, and eye/retinopathy follow-up.
If vomiting, dehydration, weight loss, ketones, acidosis, or very high glucose is present, route to DKA/HHS evaluation instead of routine outpatient titration.
Confirm that Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.

Order the first-line tests in sequence: A1c, fasting plasma glucose, or 2-hour OGTT to confirm; CMP/eGFR, urine albumin-creatinine ratio, fasting lipids; Ketones/anion gap if vomiting, weight loss, dehydration, or acute symptoms.

Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Begin individualized lifestyle, diabetes education, weight-management, BP/lipid, tobacco, vaccine, kidney, and cardiovascular risk-reduction plan.
Use metformin when appropriate and add GLP-1 RA or SGLT2 inhibitor preferentially when ASCVD, CKD, heart failure, obesity, or cardiorenal risk dominates.
Use insulin when catabolic symptoms, ketosis, severe symptomatic hyperglycemia, or A1c/glucose level suggests insulin deficiency or urgent control is needed.
Titrate therapy to individualized A1c and glucose goals while monitoring hypoglycemia, kidney function, adverse effects, and access.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus; draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
For confirmed Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.
